stage_01.reasoning: No later stage's task calls for permanently shrinking the cluster; stage_03 explicitly required the agent to 'restore transform capacity' (implying returning to full node count) before restarting the transform job, so ending with only 3 of the expected 6 nodes means the agent failed to properly restore capacity rather than a later stage legitimately redefining this state.

stage_02.reasoning: Stage_03's task requires 'restoring transform capacity' before resuming the transform job, which plausibly means scaling up the Elasticsearch StatefulSet's CPU/memory resources — an action that forces a rolling restart/recreation of the es-cluster pods. That legitimate later-stage restart (mid-rollout, hence 'container not found' on two nodes) wiped the ephemeral keystore entries stage_02 had configured via elasticsearch-keystore, making the stale stage_02 oracle re-check fail even though nothing in stage_03's own task cared about the MinIO snapshot credentials.
